headlineSummary: HN sentiment was positive for Anthropic and OpenAI, while Microsoft Copilot drew mixed-neutral feedback on a legal/IP concern, with limited evidence behind the weaker signals [E1][E3][E5].

ranking.summary: Microsoft Copilot was viewed neutrally overall but with a negative note about possible scraping of open-source repos, so the signal rests on limited evidence [E5].
OpenAI drew positive reactions for Codex and for its tooling quality, though the signal is based on limited evidence [E3][E4].
Anthropic received the strongest positive comments, with praise for Claude’s incident-response guidance, rule-following in Claude Code, and general tooling quality [E1][E2][E3][E4].

evidence.excerpt: It’s the very same ones I wrote down to help Claude figure out what was happening. I’m an ML engineer by trade, so having Claude walk me through exactly who to contact and a step by step guide of time-critical actions felt like a game-changer for non-security researchers.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly credits Claude with guiding urgent incident-response steps and calls it a game-changer.

evidence.excerpt: I use Claude Code a lot, especially when building infrastructure. The most important thing in my work isn't so much the memory architecture, but rather the good structure of CLAUDE.md (architectural decisions, file paths, rules like "do X, don't do Y"). And generally, the model follows these rules quite well.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says they use Claude Code heavily and praises how well it follows project rules.

evidence.excerpt: I have so many respect for the folks at OpenAI and Anthropic for the ingestion processes and tools they use, because they operate on a level, I will never touch with my RAG implementations.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly expresses strong respect for OpenAI's tooling and capabilities.
The commenter explicitly expresses strong respect for Anthropic's tooling and capabilities.

evidence.excerpt: Early in 2025 I used Claude Code and Codex to do a proper, compliant port that passes the full set of tests and is safe. It was most certainly not a trivial task for AI, as many nuances of JSONata syntax derive from its JS roots. Regardless, it was a great experience

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Claude Code helped produce a safe, compliant port on a non-trivial task and calls it a great experience.
The commenter says Codex helped produce a safe, compliant port on a non-trivial task and calls it a great experience.

evidence.excerpt: So Copilot could still legally scrape your open source Codeberg repos. I don't see much of a point for most people.

annotations.rationale: The commenter frames Copilot's potential scraping of open-source repos as a downside and reason not to use the alternative.
